results.title: Taṇhā sutta - Craving
results.description: The Buddha describes the three kinds of craving - 1.) craving for sensual pleasures, 2.) craving for existence, and 3.) craving for non-becoming.
results.contentSnippet: “There are these three kinds of <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">craving</mark>|, bhikkhus.::::.| What are the three? Craving for sensual pleasures

results.title: Self Erasure
results.description: A craving to end suffering through erasure of current experience or identity, e.g. “This self is unbearable; I want out.”

results.title: Sensual desire
results.description: A mental quality of desiring sensory gratification. It pulls the mind’s attention toward sights, sounds, smells, tastes, or touches in a search for satisfaction.

results.title: Self Making
results.description: Spiritual or worldly striving to “be someone,” the “I must become something” energy
synonyms: romanticizing some better self or future state

results.contentSnippet: When this was said, Moggallāna, I said this to Sakka, lord of the gods: ‘Here, lord of the gods, a bhikkhu has learned: “Nothing is worth holding on to.” When a bhikkhu has learned: “Nothing is worth

results.title: Taṇhāsaṅkhaya sutta - Extinction of Craving
results.description: Observing Aññāsi Koṇḍañña reviewing his liberation from craving, the Buddha utters an inspired verse praising the arahant who is rootless, unbound, and beyond reproach.

results.title: Rūpataṇhā sutta - Craving for Form
results.description: Craving for forms, sounds, odors, tastes, tangible objects, and mental objects is impermanent, changing, and becoming otherwise. Accepting this through faith or wisdom guarantees stream-entry.

results.title: Non Identification

synonyms: lit. not made of that state

results.title: Taṇhuppāda sutta - Uprisings of Craving
results.description: The Buddha describes the four uprisings of craving that can arise in a bhikkhu - 1.) for a robe, 2.) alms food, 3.) lodging, and 4.) for this or that state of existence.

results.title: Taṇhāsaṁyojana sutta - Fettered By Craving
results.description: The Buddha describes the fetter of craving as the most significant bond, bound by which, beings continue wandering on in cyclic existence.

results.title: Taṇhā vagga - Chapter 24 - Craving

results.title: Āhāra sutta - Nutriment
results.description: The Buddha explains the four kinds of nutriments that sustain beings that are existing and support those seeking birth, and how they arise from craving.
